Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Oct-02-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Lakritz, Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3963 for Defendant San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency.
No Other Appearance.

9:56 am.
RULING - The matter is Uncontested. Plaintiff is not present even though he indicated he would be during the previous hearing The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to all causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Joel Drotts is granted without leave to amend as to all causes of action. No opposition filed.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Joel Drotts 415-933-1354 Plaintiff in Pro Per apears late at 9:44 am.
Thomas Lakritz, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-3963 of the Offic eof the City Attorney for Defendant.

RULING - The matter is uncontested, the Court adopts the tentative ruling and signs an Order in Open Court. At 9:44 am, Plaintiff appears late.

10:04 am. The matter is recalled after Deputy City attorney has left. Plaintiff requests to continue the matter. The Court tries to call Deputy City Attorney Thomas Lakritz on the phone but was unsuccessful. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

Continued to October 2, 2018 at 9:30 am unless the date does not work for Defendant. Plaintiff gives his contact information to Deputy City Attorney Lisa Berkowitz, who is present for another matter since Deputy City Attorney Lakritz left after the first calling of the case when Plaintiff was not present.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-14-2018 Set For Trial On Feb-25-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Order To Show Cause For Preliminary Injunction. (Order To Be Served By 4-17-2018; Opposition By 4-20-2018; Reply By 4-23-2018) (See Scanned Order)
Off Calendar Apr-25-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joel Drotts, In Propria Persona, (415-933-1354) appears as self-represented plaintiff/injunction petitioner

Deputy City Attorney, Susan J. Kawala of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (415-554-3827) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's Order To Show Cause For Preliminary Injunction.

Plaintiff Joel Drotts' petition for preliminary injunction is denied. Mr. Drotts does not demonstrate a likelihood of prevailing on the merits. Mr. Drotts does not show a due process violation. The authorities he cites approve of post-tow hearings. (Goichman v. Rheuban Motors, Inc. (9th Cir. 1982) 682 F.2d 1320, 1325; Drotts' Ex Parte Petition, 52:16-21.) Mr. Drotts is unlikely to prevail on his conversion claim. Mr. Drotts admits that the nonprofit corporation owns the Lincoln. (Kawala Dec., Ex. 3.) The certificate of title that is attached to the Kawala declaration also indicates that someone other than Mr. Drotts owns the Volvo. Mr. Drotts needs an ownership interest to maintain the conversion claim. Lastly, the Kawala declaration indicates that many of the citations have not been paid and thus the tows were warranted and the balance of equities and harms does not weigh in favor of Mr. Drotts.

Written order is signed and filed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: torreanom@aol.org (L&M302)
